pattern: Slide a three-by-three window over the grid and validate each placement against the strict magic-square definition, counting those that qualify.
hints: Only three-by-three windows can be magic squares, so enumerate every such top-left corner.
A valid window must use exactly the digits one through nine, each once.
For each candidate also confirm all three rows, all three columns, and both diagonals share one common sum before counting it.

edge_inputs.description: one magic square embedded in a wider grid
grid too small to contain any window
correct sums but a repeated digit disqualifies it
distinct one-to-nine but unequal line sums
the canonical magic square exactly fills the grid
contains a value above nine, never magic
two overlapping windows, only one magic

stress.description: random grids over the allowed value range so most windows fail
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    rows = rng.randint(1, 10)
    cols = rng.randint(1, 10)
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 15) for _ in range(cols)] for _ in range(rows)]
    return [grid]
stress.note: random fills almost never form a magic square, exercising rejection
